title: [Feature]: Evaluate and stage additional observability connectors
problem: The roadmap names several possible observability destinations beyond Prometheus and OTLP. Each platform has different authentication, transport, batching, cardinality, retention, and confidentiality expectations. Implementing them ad hoc would increase dependency surface and make it harder to preserve the current policy-driven, disabled-by-default sharing model.
proposal: Create a staged connector plan for StatsD, Datadog, Splunk HEC, Elastic Observability, Grafana Alloy or Grafana Agent, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Monitoring, Amazon CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, and syslog-style bridges. The first deliverable should define a connector certification checklist, shared policy requirements, common tests, and selection criteria. Individual connector implementation issues can then be opened when priority and user demand are clear.
users: Operators with established observability platforms, security reviewers who need predictable export controls, and maintainers who need a repeatable pattern for adding connectors without weakening the sink runtime.
delivery_semantics: All connectors must remain observational only and must not participate in ACK decisions, retry decisions, DLQ routing, or sink write success. Connector failures must not stop the core runner or cause durable data loss.
security: Every connector must default to no sharing, require explicit metric allow lists, avoid sensitive labels, enforce timeouts and bounds, and document authentication and credential handling without including private details. Cloud and vendor connectors must avoid command-line secrets and must support least-privilege credentials.
acceptance: A connector evaluation matrix is documented.
A certification checklist defines required tests and security controls.
Future connector issues are created from the matrix instead of broad roadmap text.
No runtime dependency is added until a specific connector is implemented.
CHANGELOG.md records the planning and documentation change when shipped.
tests: Add documentation checks and any unit tests for shared connector policy helpers introduced by the design. Run scripts/check.sh before close-out.
documentation: Update docs/observability.md, docs/metrics.md, ROADMAP.md, and docs/security.md with the connector matrix, certification checklist, and selection guidance.
closeout: Closed after the connector evaluation created individual connector backlog items and documented the shared connector contract. Include the release tag, documented connector candidates, agreed certification criteria, and follow-up issues for selected connectors.
